description_long: This stone block is a large, rectangular monolith with a hollowed-out center featuring organically shaped, pillar-like structures connecting the top and bottom surfaces. The outer faces are smooth and flat, while the interior void is irregular, with undulating surfaces and multiple vertical supports reminiscent of natural rock formations or eroded stone. The block appears to be made of a uniform, light gray stone material, with no visible seams or joints. The openings on each side reveal the sculpted interior, allowing light to pass through and creating a sense of depth and complexity. The overall appearance is both architectural and sculptural, suggesting a modern or abstract design. There are no visible markings, engravings, or signs of significant weathering on the surface.
description: A large, rectangular stone block with smooth outer faces and an irregular, hollowed interior supported by organic, pillar-like structures.
description_view_0: Front view shows three prominent interior pillars and a wide opening revealing the sculpted void.
description_view_1: Side view highlights a single central pillar and a narrower opening, with less of the interior visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displays a similar arrangement to the front, with three interior pillars and a wide opening.
description_view_3: Opposite side view features a single central pillar and a narrow opening, mirroring view 1.
attribute_location_context: urban park or modern public plaza as a sculptural barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set on a leveled concrete or stone base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and uniformly colored, suggesting recent installation or minimal exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated, likely intended as a standalone feature
